brief_justification: Paper A's machinery is algebraic-geometric duality: mirror/3d-mirror symmetry between hyperkahler intermediate Jacobians via SYZ transforms of A- and B-branes and varying Bridgeland-type stability conditions on a derived category. Paper B's machinery is data-driven inference of a non-normal linear operator from time series, characterizing transient amplification through eigenvector non-orthogonality relative to a threshold. There is no role-for-role correspondence between a categorical brane/mirror duality and non-normal transient-growth geometry; the only overlaps ('stability', 'geometry') are homonymous keywords used in incompatible technical senses, so they do not share an underlying method.
